name: How do I figure out the right size sliding glass door for my opening?
headline: How do I figure out the right size sliding glass door for my opening?

mainEntity.name: How do I figure out the right size sliding glass door for my opening?
mainEntity.text: How do I figure out the right size sliding glass door for my opening?

acceptedAnswer.text: Finding the right size for a sliding glass door starts with **measuring the existing opening correctly** and understanding what the manufacturer’s sizes mean. For a replacement, you usually measure: - **Width**: Inside the frame from jamb to jamb at top, middle, and bottom - **Height**: From the top of the frame to the sill on both sides and in the middle You also need to check if the opening is **square** by measuring the diagonals. If one diagonal is longer, the opening is out of square, and the installer may need to adjust framing or shims. Most manufacturers offer standard sizes, but older homes, especially in areas with settling or past remodels, may not match those exactly. In those cases, you might order a **custom‑sized door** or adjust the opening to fit a standard size. Pitfalls include measuring only once, ignoring sloped or damaged sills, or ordering to the rough opening instead of the manufacturer’s recommended size. A door that’s even a bit too large or too small can be difficult or impossible to install correctly without extra work. If you’re not comfortable measuring, it’s smart to have the installer take final measurements. That way whoever measures is also responsible for getting a correctly sized door.
